questions.q: Blood pressure is highest in the ________ and falls progressively through the systemic circuit.
options: A. Capillaries
B. Veins
C. Aorta (and large elastic arteries)
D. Arterioles
E. Venules

questions.q: The innermost layer of all blood vessels, composed of simple squamous endothelium on a basement membrane, is the:
options: A. Tunica media
B. Tunica externa (adventitia)
C. Tunica intima
D. Internal elastic membrane
E. Subendothelial layer only

questions.q: The tunica media of arteries primarily consists of:
options: A. Dense irregular connective tissue with collagen only
B. Collagen and elastic fibers with no smooth muscle
C. Smooth muscle and elastic fibers that control vessel diameter (vasomotion)
D. Simple squamous epithelium
E. Adipose tissue and unmyelinated nerves

questions.q: Baroreceptors that monitor systemic arterial blood pressure are located in the:
options: A. Aortic valve and pulmonic valve
B. Aortic arch and carotid sinuses (carotid body)
C. Left ventricular myocardium
D. Pulmonary trunk
E. Hepatic portal vein

questions.q: The vasomotor center in the medulla oblongata controls blood pressure primarily by:
options: A. Adjusting heart rate through direct connections to the SA node
B. Regulating arteriolar diameter (vasoconstriction/vasodilation) via sympathetic vasomotor fibers
C. Releasing ADH to increase blood volume
D. Stimulating aldosterone secretion from the adrenal cortex directly
E. Controlling respiratory rate

questions.q: Which mechanism of venous return draws blood toward the heart by creating negative intrathoracic pressure during inspiration?
options: A. Skeletal muscle pump
B. Respiratory pump
C. Venous valves preventing backflow
D. Baroreceptor reflex
E. Sympathetic venoconstriction

questions.q: Edema (abnormal fluid accumulation in tissues) can result from:
options: A. Increased plasma oncotic pressure (high albumin)
B. Decreased capillary hydrostatic pressure
C. Increased capillary hydrostatic pressure OR decreased plasma oncotic pressure (hypoalbuminemia)
D. Increased lymphatic drainage capacity
E. Arteriolar vasoconstriction reducing capillary flow

questions.q: The three branches arising from the aortic arch, from proximal (right side) to distal (left side), are:
options: A. Left common carotid, left subclavian, brachiocephalic
B. Brachiocephalic (innominate), left common carotid, left subclavian
C. Right subclavian, right common carotid, left subclavian
D. Left subclavian, brachiocephalic, left common carotid
E. Brachiocephalic, left subclavian, left common carotid

questions.q: The hepatic portal system:
options: A. Carries oxygenated blood from the liver to the digestive organs
B. Collects nutrient-rich venous blood from the digestive organs and spleen and delivers it to the liver for processing
C. Returns blood from the liver to the inferior vena cava only
D. Is the pulmonary equivalent providing liver gas exchange
E. Connects the liver directly to the gallbladder

questions.q: Local autoregulation of blood flow to tissues involves:
options: A. Baroreceptors signaling the medullary vasomotor center
B. Sympathetic vasoconstriction as the sole mechanism
C. Intrinsic mechanisms (myogenic response and metabolic vasodilation) that match local blood flow to tissue metabolic needs
D. Hormonal control via angiotensin II exclusively
E. Neural reflexes from the hypothalamus only

questions.q: Which type of capillary, found in the liver, bone marrow, and spleen, lacks a complete basement membrane and allows passage of large proteins and blood cells?
options: A. Continuous capillaries
B. Fenestrated capillaries
C. Sinusoidal (discontinuous) capillaries
D. Lymphatic capillaries
E. Thoroughfare channel capillaries

questions.q: Vascular compliance refers to a vessel's:
options: A. Resistance to blood flow
B. Ability to stretch and accommodate volume changes (distensibility)
C. Thickness of the tunica media
D. Rate of vasoconstriction in response to sympathetic stimulation
E. Permeability to gases and small molecules

questions.q: Pulse pressure is defined as:
options: A. Mean arterial pressure
B. Diastolic blood pressure only
C. Systolic pressure minus diastolic pressure
D. The pressure within pulmonary capillaries
E. Pressure measured in the portal vein

questions.q: According to Poiseuille's law, blood vessel resistance is related to radius by:
options: A. Resistance is directly proportional to vessel radius
B. Resistance is proportional to radius squared
C. Resistance is inversely proportional to the fourth power of the radius (small changes in radius drastically alter resistance)
D. Resistance is unrelated to vessel radius
E. Resistance equals 1/radius (simple inverse relationship)

questions.q: The superior vena cava (SVC) drains venous blood from:
options: A. The abdominal and pelvic organs
B. The lower extremities and pelvis
C. The head, neck, upper extremities, and thorax
D. The lungs via the pulmonary veins
E. The liver via the hepatic veins

questions.q: Hypertension is generally defined as blood pressure of:
options: A. ≥90/60 mmHg

questions.q: The great saphenous vein is clinically important because it:
options: A. Is a deep vein of the lower limb prone to DVT
B. Is the longest vein in the body, running superficially along the medial leg and thigh, and is commonly used for bypass grafts
C. Is a branch of the femoral vein only
D. Is located on the posterior aspect of the leg
E. Drains directly into the inferior vena cava

questions.q: Mean arterial pressure (MAP) is best approximated by:
options: A. (Systolic + Diastolic) / 2
B. Diastolic pressure + 1/3 pulse pressure
C. Systolic pressure − diastolic pressure
D. Diastolic pressure alone
E. Pulse pressure × 2

questions.q: Vasodilation of arterioles is promoted by all of the following EXCEPT:
options: A. Decreased local O2 tension
B. Increased CO2 and H+ (local acidity)
C. Sympathetic adrenergic nervous system stimulation (causes vasoconstriction via alpha receptors)
D. Nitric oxide (NO) release from endothelium
E. Increased local tissue temperature

questions.q: At the arterial end of a capillary, net filtration of fluid into the tissues occurs when:
options: A. Plasma oncotic pressure exceeds capillary hydrostatic pressure
B. Capillary hydrostatic pressure exceeds plasma oncotic (colloid osmotic) pressure
C. Lymphatic drainage is increased
D. Arteriolar pressure falls below venous pressure
E. Venous pressure increases enough to reverse flow

questions.q: The circle of Willis is an arterial anastomosis located:
options: A. Supplying the abdominal viscera via the celiac trunk
B. At the base of the brain, connecting carotid and vertebral arterial territories
C. In the coronary circulation around the heart
D. In the hepatic portal circulation
E. Connecting the brachial and radial arteries at the elbow

questions.q: A thrombus is:
options: A. A blood clot that detaches and travels in the bloodstream
B. A stationary blood clot that forms within an intact blood vessel
C. An air bubble traveling in the bloodstream
D. A fat particle that causes arterial obstruction
E. Increased platelet count in circulation

questions.q: An embolus differs from a thrombus in that an embolus:
options: A. Is always composed of a blood clot fragment only
B. Is stationary within a vessel at the site of formation
C. Is any material (clot fragment, fat globule, air bubble, amniotic fluid) traveling in the bloodstream
D. Occurs only in arteries, never veins
E. Is identical to an atherosclerotic plaque

questions.q: The renal arteries are direct paired branches of the:
options: A. Superior mesenteric artery
B. Abdominal aorta
C. Celiac trunk
D. Common iliac artery
E. Inferior mesenteric artery

questions.q: Varicose veins result from:
options: A. Arterial wall thickening due to atherosclerosis
B. Incompetent venous valves allowing blood to pool, causing the veins to dilate and become tortuous
C. Rupture of capillaries under high pressure
D. Arterioles losing smooth muscle tone
E. Increased blood viscosity

questions.q: The external iliac artery continues as the ________ artery after passing under the inguinal ligament.
options: A. Internal iliac
B. Femoral
C. Popliteal
D. Obturator
E. Tibial

questions.q: The pulmonary circulation differs from the systemic circulation in that:
options: A. It operates at higher blood pressure than the systemic circuit
B. Pulmonary arteries carry deoxygenated blood and pulmonary veins carry oxygenated blood
C. Pulmonary arteries carry oxygenated blood directly to the lungs
D. It is driven by the left ventricle
E. It does not involve capillary exchange

questions.q: Angiogenesis (formation of new capillaries from pre-existing vessels) is stimulated primarily by:
options: A. Increased arterial blood pressure
B. Tissue hypoxia triggering release of VEGF (vascular endothelial growth factor)
C. Sympathetic nervous system activation
D. Decreased blood viscosity
E. Elevated plasma protein concentration

questions.q: Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) most commonly occurs in the deep veins of the:
options: A. Upper extremities and neck
B. Hepatic portal system
C. Lower extremities (especially calf and thigh)
D. Pulmonary veins
E. Coronary veins

questions.q: Atrial natriuretic peptide (ANP), released by atrial myocytes in response to increased blood volume stretching the atria, acts to:
options: A. Stimulate aldosterone secretion and Na+ retention
B. Promote sodium and water reabsorption by the kidneys
C. Inhibit renin and aldosterone secretion, promoting Na+ and water excretion to lower blood pressure and volume
D. Activate the renin-angiotensin system
E. Stimulate ADH release from the posterior pituitary
